rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for interface assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should define input assumptions and output guarantees using visible ports and constants.
Implementation annexes should contain bindings and assignments that reference subcomponents and connections.
Avoid introducing package-qualified names or types not present in the input AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must be placed in valid AADL scopes: type for interface behavior, implementation for subcomponent-level details.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes or vice versa.
Do not reference non-visible or non-existent identifiers in AGREE annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
